channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: Russia-China naval drills not a threat to other nations: Kremlin
item.description: The Kremlin said that joint naval drills ​between Russia and China which kicked off on Monday ‌in the waters and airspace off the Chinese city of Qingdao were not directed against any other country ​and would boost regional security. The annual drills ​are due to run from July 6 ⁠to 13 and Ru Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Spain expects to welcome 100 million tourists this year
item.description: Spain is likely to attract 100 million ​foreign tourists this year with the number of visitors expected to grow during the ‌key summer season helped by uncertainty in the Middle East, Tourism Minister Jordi Hereu told reporters on Monday. The world's second-most-visited country after France w Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: UK minister leaving X over ‘abuse, misinformation’
item.description: Britain's culture minister Lisa Nandy said on Thursday she had decided to leave Elon Musk's social media platform X, formerly Twitter, saying it now "favours abuse and misinformation over meaningful debate". "I've decided to leave this platform and my department will too," Nandy said in a post on Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Spain attributes over 1,000 excess deaths to heat in second-hottest June ever
item.description: Spain recorded 1,029 excess deaths last month attributable to heat, official data showed on Wednesday, as a five-day heatwave with temperatures surpassing 40 degrees Celsius made it the second-hottest month of June on record. Data on the Health Ministry's daily mortality monitoring system MoMo, sh Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: France records 1,000 excess deaths during record-breaking heatwave
item.description: France has recorded 1,000 excess deaths during the blistering heatwave sweeping Europe, the public health agency said on Sunday, warning that the true figure was likely to be higher. Detailing its preliminary count of excess deaths, Sante Publique said most of the fatalities involved older people Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Death toll in Venezuela earthquakes tops 1,400
item.description: The death toll from Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes rose above 1,400 on Saturday as foreign rescue teams poured into the country and authorities pressed on with the search for survivors in the hardest-hit coastal areas. The updated toll came as rescuers fanned out across La Guaira and par Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Ukraine has attacked bus carrying schoolchildren: Russia
item.description: Russia accused Ukraine of conducting a deadly drone strike on a bus carrying Belarusian schoolchildren on Wednesday, an allegation that ​Ukraine's military said was "false". Yegor Kovalchuk, the acting governor of Russia's ‌Bryansk region, which borders Ukraine, said the bus had been taking a Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Italian mother takes on Meta, TikTok after daughter's death
item.description:
In the span of just a few months, Irene Roggero Ugues watched her daughter Rossella's behaviour change as social media fed her an increasing stream of self-harm content, before the 12-year-old died by suicide.
Only after Rossella's death did Irene and her husband unlock her devices. They f Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Russian artist known as Putin critic shot dead in eastern Poland
item.description: A Russian artist and cartoonist known for mocking Russian President Vladimir Putin was shot dead in eastern Poland on Monday in what Polish media are describing as a possible "execution-style" killing, reports aa.com.tr. The victim has been identified by several Polish broadcasters as Semyon Skrep Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: French watchdog orders conservative news channel CNews to comply with pluralism rules
item.description: France's media ‌regulator on Monday ordered rolling news channel CNews to comply with rules on pluralism and diversity of opinion, putting the ultra-conservative channel owned by billionaire Vincent Bollore one ​step away from disciplinary action. The channel, which critics have likened to th Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: German finance minister proposes income tax cuts, Spiegel reports
item.description: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil has presented coalition negotiators with two proposals for ​an income tax reform, including tax ‌relief of up to €20 billion ($21.6 billion) and a higher levy on top earners, Spiegel reported on Monday.One ​proposal would provide moderate relief for Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Switzerland votes on proposal to cap population at 10 million
item.description: Swiss voters decide on Sunday whether to back a proposal to cap the country's population in a referendum likened to Britain's Brexit vote, which could have far-reaching consequences for the economy and Bern's relations with the European Union. Driven by concern about immigration, pressure on publi Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Russia tries to jam Starlink systems to counter Ukrainian drones
item.description: Russian forces are trying to counter Ukrainian "mid-strike" drone attacks by camouflaging cargoes and installing powerful jamming systems to disrupt Elon Musk's Starlink satellite internet system, Ukrainian drone commanders and pilots told ​Reuters. Kyiv's development of "mid-strike" drones that c Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: How a Palestinian town is defending itself from Israeli settler attacks
item.description: On a cool night in June, some 15 Palestinians from the town of Sinjil in the occupied West Bank gathered on a hilltop to watch the shadowed valleys below for any sign of movement ​that might signal an impending Israeli settler attack. They are part of a grassroots volunteer group — similar t Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Four die in Delhi building collapse as rains batter India
item.description:
At least four people died in a building collapse in the Indian capital ​Delhi, the latest in a series of ‌rain-related incidents as monsoon showers triggered landslides and disrupted travel across parts of the country. Here are some details:
One ​person was rescued alive from the building Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: TMC loses grip on bank accounts with Rs 4.4bn
item.description: In the latest blow for Mamata Banerjee, Indian authorities have frozen three Trinamool Congress (TMC) bank accounts with deposits worth Rs 4.4 billion amid investigations into alleged money laundering. The Enforcement Directorate (ED) said the HDFC Bank accounts were frozen under the anti-money la Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Death toll from Venezuela quakes rises to 3,811 as government seeks frozen funds
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: US military carries out fresh strikes on Iran
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Lula leads Bolsonaro in Brazil presidential run-off, poll shows
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Venezuela's opposition leader Machado says she will return to Venezuela to help quake victims
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Vance arrives in Switzerland for Iran peace talks with Hormuz in spotlight
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: State of emergency declared in Bolivia
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump's $100,000 H-1B visa fee is unlawful, US judge rules
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump appoints ally and FHFA chief Pulte as acting US intelligence director
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Cargo plane goes missing off Pakistan's coast
item.description: A cargo plane with five crew members aboard went missing off the Pakistani port of Karachi late Tuesday after rapidly descending and losing contact with air traffic controllers on a flight from the United Arab Emirates, officials said. Pakistan’s airport authority posted on X that search and Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: 30 Pakistan Coast Guards personnel killed in suicide bombing
item.description: The Baloch Liberation Army (BLA) has claimed responsibility for a major attack on a Pakistan Coast Guards camp in the Panwan area of Jiwani, Gwadar district, alleging that more than 30 security personnel were killed and dozens injured in what it described as a "fidayeen" (suicide) operation. In a Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Mamata faces fresh blow as new TMC Bengal chief Chandrima quits
item.description: Amid continued setbacks and rebellion involving Trinamool Congress (TMC) since the last assembly election, the party chief in the Indian state of West Bengal has resigned. Chandrima Bhattacharya, a former minister and three-time MLA in West Bengal and long considered one of the party chief Mamata Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Iran's slain leader Khamenei laid in state in Tehran for week of mass funeral events
item.description: The body of Iran's slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was lying in state in a vast hall in Tehran on Friday as clerics, officials, foreign dignitaries and other mourners paid their respects after his 37-year rule. Iran is staging a week of mass funeral processions for Khamenei -- killed i Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Instagram running ads promoting child sexual abuse material in India, BBC finds
item.description: Instagram has been running paid adverts promoting child sexual abuse material in India, a BBC Eye investigation has found.The ads, seen by the BBC World Service, use terms including "rape video" and "child video" and link users to channels on the messaging app Telegram, where they can buy the mater Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Sri Lanka mulls pharmacy sale of drug test kits
item.description: Sri Lanka is considering introducing drug test kits through pharmacies to allow parents to privately check whether their children are using narcotics, the President's Media Division (PMD) said. The proposal was discussed when the national council of the "A Nation United" anti-drug campaign, launch Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Forty killed as bus falls into ditch in Pakistan
item.description: Forty people ​were killed and ‌eight others injured after a bus ​fell into ​a deep roadside ⁠ditch in Pakistan's ​southwestern province ​of Balochistan on Friday, a rescue agency ​and a ​government official said. The bus ‌was ⁠headed to the national capital, Islamabad, from the provincia Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Powerful Iranian general emerges before Khamenei funeral
item.description: A powerful general who leads Iran's paramilitary Revolutionary Guard emerged from hiding as Tehran prepared Friday for the dayslong funeral for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Photos published online by Iranian state media showed Gen. Ahmad Vahidi attending a meeting about the fune Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Seven killed in India as bus hits trailer, goes up in flames
item.description: At least seven people have been killed and 22 others injured after a sleeper bus crashed into a trailer and caught on fire on the Delhi-Mumbai Expressway in India’s Rajasthan, reports bdnews24.com citing NDTV. The incident took place around 2:30am local time on Wednesday when the bus w Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Saudi Aramco helicopter crash kills 14
item.description: A helicopter belonging to Saudi oil giant Aramco crashed on Sunday in Ras Tanura on Saudi Arabia's eastern coast on the Gulf, west of the Strait of Hormuz, killing 14 nationals, the state news agency reported, adding that the cause was unknown. Aramco had resumed crude oil loadings on Friday at it Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Eight killed in Pakistani military operations in Balochistan
item.description: Pakistan’s military says that it has killed eight “terrorists” in intelligence operations in Balochistan’s Kharan and Mastung districts, reports Pakistani daily Dawn. The country’s Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR) said it had conducted the “intelligence-dr Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: 700 civilians killed by Myanmar Army in 6 months
item.description: Myanmar's military killed more than 700 civilians in six months, including hundreds of women and children, according to a UN report cited by the BBC. The report from the UN Human Rights Office, covering August to January -- the six months from when the military announced elections -- verified a mi Delivered by PolitePaul service
